failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Tenant API key authentication bypass via dict timing attack
failure_scenarios.evidence: Line 340-345: api_keys dict lookup is O(1) but timing varies based on key presence. Attacker can measure response time to enumerate valid API keys. No constant-time comparison implemented.

Line 175-185: Plugin loading uses importlib.import_module() with no sandboxing. Malicious plugin can import os, subprocess, etc. and execute arbitrary code on host. No seccomp, no capability restrictions, no network isolation.

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Tenant quota bypass via race condition
failure_scenarios.evidence: Lines 295-330: check_quota() and record_usage() are separate async calls without atomic transaction. Concurrent requests can pass quota check simultaneously before either records usage. Classic TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) vulnerability.

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Auto-deploy without human review for critical fixes
failure_scenarios.evidence: Lines 245-260: Auto-fix allowed for confidence >= 0.9 AND severity low/medium. Attacker could craft issues with high confidence score that trigger auto-deploy of malicious code. No code signing, no hash verification, no rollback testing before deploy.

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Cross-project data leakage via shared insights
failure_scenarios.evidence: Lines 95-150: Insights extracted from ALL projects without tenant isolation. In multi-tenant SaaS, Tenant A's patterns could leak to Tenant B. No tenant_id filtering in _load_all_traces().

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Memory exhaustion via unbounded plugin hook list
failure_scenarios.evidence: Lines 215-230: hooks dict stores List[Callable] with no maximum. Malicious plugin could register thousands of hooks for same event, causing memory exhaustion and slow execution. No rate limiting on plugin registration.

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Silent failure in plugin hook execution
failure_scenarios.evidence: Lines 220-225: Exception in plugin hook caught and logged, but execution continues silently. Security-critical hooks (VALIDATION) could fail without alerting anyone. No escalation on hook failure.
